persona.name: Jordan

initialIntent:
## Purpose
Create a patient intake form for our orthopedic clinic with insurance details and medical history focused on musculoskeletal conditions.

## Audience
Patients visiting the orthopedic clinic for the first time.

## Constraints
Must capture insurance provider and policy details. Pain assessment using a standardized scale is required.
finalIntent:
# Orthopedic Clinic Patient Intake Form

## Form Purpose
This form collects comprehensive patient information for first-time visitors to an orthopedic clinic. It captures personal details, insurance information, and musculoskeletal-focused medical history to facilitate efficient patient care and billing processes.

## Target Audience
New patients scheduling their first appointment at the orthopedic clinic, typically individuals experiencing joint, bone, muscle, or connective tissue issues.

## Key Data Collection Requirements

### Personal Information
- Full name, date of birth, contact details
- Emergency contact information

### Insurance Details
- Insurance provider name
- Policy number and group number
- Policyholder information
- Insurance type (primary/secondary)

### Medical History (Musculoskeletal Focus)
- Current chief complaint and affected body area
- Pain assessment using standardized 0-10 numeric scale
- Previous orthopedic injuries or surgeries
- Current medications
- Relevant medical conditions affecting bones/joints
- Activity level and functional limitations

## Validation Requirements
- All insurance fields are mandatory
- Pain scale must use standardized 0-10 rating
- Contact information must be valid and complete
- Date fields must be properly formatted
schemaDescription: 30 fields: First Name*, Last Name*, Date of Birth*, Email Address*, Phone Number*, Street Address*, City*, State*, ZIP Code*, Emergency Contact Name*, Emergency Contact Phone*, Relationship to Patient*, Insurance Provider*, Policy Number*, Group Number, Policyholder Name*, Relationship to Policyholder*, Insurance Type*, Chief Complaint*, Affected Body Area*
